# NotebookTools

> NotebookTools is a free Chrome extension that imports YouTube videos, ChatGPT and Gemini conversations, X (Twitter) posts, webpages, and selected text directly into Google NotebookLM.

NotebookTools helps researchers, students, and knowledge workers capture web content into NotebookLM without copy-paste. It uses your existing Google sign-in — no separate account, no backend server, and no analytics.

## What it does

- **YouTube**: Adds an import button on watch pages. Choose a notebook and add the video as a source in one click.
- **ChatGPT & Gemini**: Adds a NotebookLM button at the top of chats. Imports the full conversation as clean text.
- **X / Twitter**: Imports threads and articles as text sources.
- **Webpages**: Import any page URL from the extension popup.
- **Selected text**: Right-click highlighted text on any site → Add to NotebookLM.

## Key facts

- **Price**: Free — all features included.
- **Platform**: Google Chrome (Manifest V3 extension).
- **Auth**: Uses your signed-in NotebookLM / Google session in the browser.
- **Privacy**: No backend server; no analytics; data stays between your browser and NotebookLM.
- **Affiliation**: Independent tool — not affiliated with Google.

## Pages

- Chrome Web Store / source: https://github.com/rafeequemv1/notebooktools

## Common questions

**What is NotebookTools?**
A free Chrome extension that sends videos, chats, posts, pages, and highlights into NotebookLM.

**Is NotebookTools free?**
Yes. Every feature is free with no paywall.

**Does NotebookTools work with ChatGPT and Gemini?**
Yes. In-page buttons extract and import full conversations.

**How do I import a YouTube video to NotebookLM?**
Install NotebookTools, open NotebookLM once to sign in, visit a YouTube watch page, click the NotebookLM button, and pick a notebook.

**Is my data sent to NotebookTools servers?**
No. The extension talks directly to NotebookLM using your browser session.

## Contact

Project repository: https://github.com/rafeequemv1/notebooktools
